currentFacts: Electron desktop pet platform with Control Center
PetService is the single source of truth for pet state
Control Center owns user-facing configuration
API keys stay in the main-process secret store

AI chat provider settings now separate active saved config from renderer drafts, support separate save and test connection actions, return structured sanitized diagnostics, optional /models discovery, provider-aware model compatibility hints, and protect credentialed Base URLs from renderer display downgrades; common OpenAI/OpenRouter/Together/LM Studio/vLLM/local provider presets are endpoint templates that still require save plus connection test or health check, while only the OpenPet 8317 gateway preset is tied to the archived OpenPet gateway smoke evidence

Host-owned image-generation model settings are available for Creator Studio through one OpenAI-compatible image Provider contract with main-process API key storage through SecretService, health checks, optional /models discovery, provider-aware compatibility hints, and host-owned output writes; legacy fixture/cloud/local plugin inputs are accepted only as compatibility read aliases and normalize into the unified provider path

Third-party ecosystem docs frame packages as local extensions with legacy SDK compatibility, explicit setup execution for enabled policy-allowed local plugins, entries.commands support through the existing JavaScript compatibility runner plus explicit short-lived process execution for declaration-only local extensions, short-lived command bridge access for pet.say/pet.action/pet.event/read-only context, host-owned OPENPET_DATA_DIR/OPENPET_CACHE_DIR/OPENPET_LOG_DIR injection for declaration-only command runs, creator-tools action reads/validation/bounded writes plus active installed user pack metadata workflows, package-local frame inspection/import, and user-approved picker frame inspection/import through the same short-lived bridge, command result feedback in Control Center, explicit HTTP/HTTPS dashboard opening, explicit service start/stop controls without auto-start, manual loopback-only service health checks, opt-in host-managed periodic health checks for running services, command/setup/service spawning without shell expansion, best-effort process-group cleanup on service stop, exit-confirmed setup/command/service stop semantics, bounded host-side force stop for stubborn services, host-owned process-tree fallback cleanup across explicit service/setup/declaration-command stop paths, controlled-host plugin cleanup evidence collection, cleanup evidence helper generation, cleanup evidence runner archives, packaged-app cleanup evidence runner archives, packaged-app cleanup evidence runner archives, cleanup evidence archive manifests, structured plugin cleanup readiness reports with validation-first updates, separate maintainer approval rehearsal artifacts for submission bundles, existing-plugin real-world submission rehearsal evidence, remote-source submission rehearsal evidence, community-source candidate discovery reporting, community-source invitation kits, community-source candidate intake reporting, compatible-intake-to-submission bridge tooling, community-source submission evidence tooling, and honest non-sandbox language; live bridge docs and tests now pin the implemented route and permission boundary including trigger-proposals:write for Creator Studio review handoff and model:image-generate for host-managed settings, health checks, and bounded image generation
Local HTTP and MCP are loopback-only and disabled by default
Codex pet directory and zip import are supported
Bundled pets include doro, duodong, and chispa

TypeScript migration baseline covers shared IPC, Control Center view contracts, the Control Center API facade, Control Center hook state boundaries, Control Center pane prop surfaces, main-process Control Center adapters for service/catalog/plugin/pet pack/About/update/actions payloads, plugin extension entry contracts, plugin submission evidence contracts, community-source invitation evidence contracts, plugin cleanup archive/runner contracts, packaged plugin cleanup evidence contracts, AI provider smoke report contracts, Creator Studio provider smoke report contracts, macOS release evidence summary/archive contracts, Windows smoke report/evidence summary/archive contracts, desktop picker smoke report contracts, desktop picker evidence summary/archive contracts, packaged runtime smoke report/evidence contracts, full release evidence archive / signed closure report contracts, and representative product payload fixtures

Packaged runtime smoke evidence is archived under docs/release-evidence/packaged-runtime/2026-06-16T14-52-13-074Z-darwin-arm64/: it proves an unsigned macOS packaged runtime launched, rendered transparently, played built-in pack actions, and restored stable state, but plugin-picker-evidence-linked and pet-picker-evidence-linked remain pending and invalid-package-feedback remains blocked until a reviewed desktop picker smoke report is linked; Phase 65 requires packaged runtime readiness plus archive readiness to link the paired desktop picker smoke report, Phase 66 adds desktop picker evidence summary/archive manifest tooling for reviewed picker archives, Phase 67 requires release-level archive and signed closure flows to consume that reviewed picker archive manifest, and Phase 81 requires release-level archive and signed closure flows to consume a matching reviewed Windows smoke archive manifest

Signed release closure evidence is archived under docs/release-evidence/signed-release-closure/2026-06-16T15-00-00Z/: releaseReady is false and official desktop, macOS, and Windows claims are not-ready; missing signed macOS codesign/notarization/Gatekeeper evidence, missing desktop picker evidence, unsigned packaged runtime evidence, missing signed Windows smoke evidence, Apple signing/notarization credentials, real Windows signed artifact execution, and human evidence review remain release blockers or Manual-required prerequisites
Current legacy plugin config is public settings; secret-like plugin config fields are rejected by the compatibility validator
Legacy SDK scaffolding is available through npm run create-openpet-plugin, including minimal, network, storage, and AI-assisted templates

Extension author docs are centered on plugin.json entries for setup, commands, services, dashboards, manifest declarations, optional config, and assets; current runtime explicitly runs setup entries for enabled policy-allowed local plugins, supports entries.commands through JavaScript compatibility packages with main, runs declaration-only local entries.commands as explicit short-lived processes with JSON stdin context, shows entry declarations and the latest command result summary in Control Center, injects short-lived bridge URL/token plus host-owned data/cache/log env vars for declaration-only commands, exposes creator-tools action reads/validation/bounded writes plus active installed user pack metadata workflows, package-local frame inspection/import, user-approved picker frame inspection/import, reviewed action trigger proposal acceptance through trigger-proposals:write, and host-mediated image-generation model bridge access through model:image-generate on the same short-lived bridge, explicitly opens declared HTTP/HTTPS dashboards for enabled plugins, explicitly starts/stops declared service entries for enabled local plugins without auto-start, manually checks declared loopback service health endpoints, can host-manage periodic health checks for already running services through Control Center, spawns command/setup/service processes without shell expansion, attempts best-effort process-group cleanup on stop, only reports setup/command/service stop completion after child exit confirmation, adds one bounded host-side force-stop attempt for stubborn services, tries host-owned process-tree fallback cleanup across explicit service/setup/declaration-command stop paths, adds controlled-host cleanup evidence collection, cleanup evidence helper generation, cleanup evidence runner archives, packaged-app cleanup evidence runner archives, packaged-app cleanup evidence runner archives, cleanup evidence archive manifests, and structured cleanup readiness reports with validation-first updates, adds separate maintainer approval rehearsal artifacts beside ready-for-review submission bundles, includes an existing-plugin real-world submission rehearsal path, a remote-source rehearsal path, a community-source discovery path, a community-source invitation kit path, a community-source candidate intake path, a compatible-intake-to-submission bridge, a real public adjacent-candidate intake archive for alvinunreal/openpets, and a community-source submission evidence path for examples such as weather-status; plugin-managed provider credentials remain unsupported when an extension uses OpenPet's host-managed generation surface, and keeps raw filesystem grants, general pet-pack writes, plugin-selected output paths, random/state/event trigger-rule persistence, and universal process-tree cleanup guarantees as future work
Plugin sandbox evaluation recommends keeping the current child-process Node permission-model VM runner for v1.1 while documenting that it is not an absolute sandbox
Installed user pet packs can be exported as .openpet-pet.zip and re-imported with provenance metadata
AI behavior decisions are visible in Control Center with replay, redacted diagnostics export, and clear-history controls

Creator Studio owns prompts, GenerationTask normalization, run workspaces, QA, preview, review state, retry/recover flow, and structured import handoff guidance; OpenPet host owns model secrets, provider calls, output writes, pet/action import, and final trigger persistence

AI provider smoke evidence for the user's OpenPet development gateway is archived under docs/release-evidence/ai-provider-smoke/2026-06-28T11-08-10Z-openpet-gateway/: /models exposed gpt-5.5 and gpt-image-2, gpt-5.5 passed chat completion smoke, image generation remained intentionally opt-in and was skipped, and the archived report keeps the API key redacted

Creator Studio provider smoke evidence for the user's OpenPet development gateway is archived under docs/release-evidence/creator-studio-provider-smoke/2026-06-28T14-06-27-403Z/: the saved host-owned gpt-image-2 configuration passed health check, returned one source PNG after about 265s using requested 512x512 generation constraints, and passed 16-frame action QA with zero warnings under a temporary 420000ms timeout override; that archive proves provider-path correctness, not production asset-quality approval

Provider-backed Creator Studio full-pet runs now build a real generated atlas, write source-image-validation.json and atlas-validation.json, and gate Import Approved Pet on passing QA before host import runs

Creator Studio action imports now submit trigger proposals into the host-owned Trigger Proposal Inbox for review; click can update clickAction, manual and unbound stay acknowledgements, and random/state/event proposals create active host-owned durable trigger rules
Live documentation has a docs/README.md map and is consolidated so README, HANDOFF, project-status-review, project-context, and v1.1 TODO status share the same current facts
The live code-aligned TODO index lives in docs/openpet-current-todo-architecture.md
The Phase 38+ execution design lives in docs/productization-v1.1-todo-design.md

releaseNotes.humanFacing: Keep README and CHANGELOG short, factual, and free of phase-by-phase history.
releaseNotes.machineFacing: Use this file plus docs/project-documentation-design.md for quick programmatic context.
